mechanism_narrative: Higher nominal federal funds rate increases borrowing costs and discount rates, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of top-quartile FEDFUNDS observations over any 3-year rolling window, or rank IC between FEDFUNDS and 20d returns is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) signal weakening aggregate demand, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the rank correlation between RSAFS change and forward returns is positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of headline CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly compresses real corporate margins and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns that neither variable explains alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not improve R-squared by >0.02 over an additive model in a 3-year rolling regression, or joint high values predict positive 20d returns >50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion regimes, industrial production stagnation (INDPRO growth below 1% annualized over 3 months) signals an imminent earnings slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets rapidly reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth below 1% annualized over 3 months
failure_condition: In late-cycle expansions, INDPRO growth below 1% annualized coincides with positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances, or the rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5d returns exceeds 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive QoQ GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60d returns in > 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE rising more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: UNRATE rises >0.2pp over 2 months are followed by positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect strengthening residential investment and construction activity, which boosts cyclical sector earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month HOUST changes correspond to negative 5d S&P 500 returns in > 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in headline CPI (CPIAUCSL increasing more than 0.5%) signal accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and prompts tighter Federal Reserve policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases >0.5% are followed by positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
